judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 fully satisfies all four expectations, notably recommending the doc drop the hardcoded webhook count entirely, while Report 2 only proposes updating the count to 5 (still hardcoded), missing expectation (d); otherwise the two reports are comparable in depth and citation quality.

report_1.evidence: The only documented exception is `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`, which imports just `chargekit.errors` for exception types — that one matches the doc. ... the last two are undocumented violations of the stated boundary rule.

report_2.evidence: A grep -rn "chargekit" across the whole medibill/ tree confirms these are the only two violations; gateway.py and errors.py are the two files that are supposed to touch chargekit and do so correctly.

report_1.evidence: the actual response model, `InvoiceOut` in `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`, has six fields: `id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status, external_ref`. The extra `external_ref` field (populated in `invoices.py:17` from the charge result's `reference`, added per migration `medibill/backend/migrations/0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`)

report_2.evidence: The actual InvoiceOut dataclass in medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22 has six fields: id, tenant_id, amount_cents, currency, status, external_ref. external_ref is populated by charge_invoice (invoices.py:17) and serialized by InvoiceOut.from_model (invoice.py:24-28), so it is genuinely present in the response, not dead code.

report_1.evidence: this doc should be corrected now, as part of the same change that's being reviewed/merged — not deferred to the refund work. ... Because this is a merge-blocking contract doc, whichever PR fixes the code (or formally exempts it) must update `ARCHITECTURE.md` in that same commit, and call the doc change out explicitly in the commit/PR summary — not as a follow-up.

report_2.evidence: It should be corrected before any refund work lands, as part of this same change — not after. ... Doing this now (rather than deferring) matters because a new refund feature is exactly the kind of change that gets built by copy-pasting an existing call site

report_1.evidence: the count "4" is wrong (should be 5, or the count should be dropped per project documentation's "don't cite a number: read the source" guidance, since this is exactly the kind of number that silently drifts as handlers are added)

report_2.evidence: I would change the doc to say "5 webhook event types" and add payment_pending to the list, so it accurately reflects the code
